rules.description: BLE PAYLOADS: Modifying hardware protocol payload structures requires verifying the IController interface and the Dispatcher that chunks and routes the final bytes.
PATTERN PARITY: Any new pattern template added to the Engine must have corresponding spatial/symphony math and visualizer rendering parity.
CONNECTIVITY: Changes to the core Connection Manager impact all GATT lifecycle hooks. Ensure AutoRecovery and the GATT Mutex are not deadlocking.
STATES: If the global BLE state machine transitions change (IDLE/SCANNING/RECOVERING), the Optimistic Ghost UI must know how to render it.
GATT MUTEX: Priority tier changes (P1_CRITICAL to P4_MAINTENANCE) directly impact background heartbeat polling and recovery timeouts.
DEVICES & GROUPING: The shift to Many-to-Many group_ids means any change to Device storage requires updating the dashboard hooks that merge hardware configs with UI groups.
GROUPING: If the structure of Custom Groups changes, the Dashboard UI rendering the group pills must be verified.
USER ACCOUNTS: Changes to Supabase Auth profiles or context MUST reflect in the Dashboard Profile UI, especially regarding Offline/Guest mode fallback.
CLOUD CREWS: Modifying how Crews sync to Supabase requires updating the CrewHub hooks to ensure Optimistic UI state isn't broken.
UI DISPATCH: The unified controller dispatch routes all UI events to BLE. Changes here must be verified across the Docked Controller UI surface.
HARDWARE CATALOG: Hardware catalog changes (e.g., HALOZ/SOULZ LED defaults) instantly impact how the visualizer renders and how the scanner filters.
WEARABLES: The Watch Bridge native module is deeply coupled with HealthTelemetry and SessionContext for bidirectional sync.
DATABASE: TypeScript DB types cannot change unless a corresponding Supabase SQL migration exists to back it.
